weather_line: Fog lifting by nine · a perfect fifth heard in the east wing

stories.headline: Briefcase Opens: Contents Confirmed as 'The Thing'; Ministry Immediately Denies Everything
stories.byline: By our Level Four correspondent, filing from a safe distance
body: At approximately half past ten this morning, in what witnesses are calling 'the least surprising surprise of the decade,' the glowing briefcase opened of its own accord in the Level Four corridor of the Ministry of Magic — and the contents were revealed to be precisely what the recovered memo had always called them: the thing. Observers report a warm gold light, a brief chord of extraordinary harmonic perfection, and the distinct sensation that everything was going to be fine.

The gold-ribboned envelope, which had been sitting beside the case since Tuesday, unfolded itself neatly and was found to contain a single card, addressed to Christine by name and written in the same smug, elegant hand. The card read: 'You were owed this. It took us long enough. — The Management.' The Ministry has issued a statement denying any knowledge of any briefcase, any envelope, any vending machine, any memo filed behind any radiator, and, relatedly, any radiator.

Christine accepted the contents with the composure of someone who had frankly expected something like this eventually, signed one form, and was back at her desk by eleven. The Niffler who first flagged the case has been given a commendation and a small biscuit. The vending machine dispensed a round of applause, entirely unsolicited, and has since gone quiet.

stories.headline: Steve Completes Trail Preparations; Jeep Reportedly 'Absolutely Ready' and Refuses to Play It Cool
body: With Thursday's trail now one day away, Steve has completed all preparations for the expedition toward the northeast trail named — if the duck-map cartographers are to be believed — Hollow Crown Ridge. The Jeep has received fresh enchantment checks, a thorough look-over, and what Steve describes as 'a talk.' All four tyres have been confirmed mud-willing. The enchanted winch has been re-spooled. The fog lamps were found to already be on, which Steve has elected not to investigate.

The Jeep itself, for its part, is visibly struggling to contain its enthusiasm. It was observed this morning idling gently in the drive without being asked, and it has twice inched toward the road when nobody was looking. When Christine walked past it this evening carrying her trail bag, witnesses report it made a small, eager sound. Steve reminded it sternly that 'it's tomorrow,' and it settled — though the dashboard compass is reportedly pointing northeast regardless of orientation, which Steve is calling 'a known issue.'

stories.headline: Duck Bandit Identified: A Figure Emerges at the Edge of the Drive; The Case Is Nearly Closed
body: After four days of charmed ducks, one birthday hat, one tiny travelling cloak, and one authenticated cartographic document, the identity of the Duck Bandit appears — at last — to be within reach. A figure was observed in the early hours near the Jeep: small, unhurried, possessed of remarkable tyre-cleaning ability, and carrying what onlookers describe as 'something rubbery and probably yellow.' The figure departed before being clearly seen, leaving behind an eighth duck.

The eighth duck is dressed in an explorer's hat and holds, in its small rubber wing, a folded note containing only a set of map coordinates and the words: 'Tomorrow. I'll be there.' Steve has triangulated the coordinates. They match Hollow Crown Ridge precisely. Christine, reviewing the evidence with the calm of someone who out-negotiates goblins before breakfast, has declared the Bandit 'known enough for now' and confirmed the expedition departs at dawn.

stories.headline: Household Governance Report: Lucy Audited; Ellie Befriends a Spoon; Jersey Holds a Summit on the Sofa
stories.byline: Our creature correspondent, embedded in the sitting room
body: In a household already charged with Ministry revelations and expedition anticipation, the three familiars have, admirably, kept themselves busy. Sources close to the biscuit tin report no new incidents — which, experienced observers note, is precisely what Lucy would want everyone to believe.

Jersey convened what he appears to regard as an important diplomatic gathering on the sofa's warmest cushion at midday, attended by himself, a blanket, and two throw pillows who he clearly considers allies. The summit produced no communiqué but is understood to have resolved several pressing issues of comfort.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Audit inconclusive. Evidence, as always, insufficient. Lucy has offered to chair the investigation committee, and considers this entirely appropriate.
Ellie: Became briefly convinced that a serving spoon was a new friend, greeted it warmly, and waited patiently for it to greet her back. Eventually decided it was simply shy. No hard feelings.
Jersey: The summit concluded successfully. Jersey has extended a formal offer of co-habitation to the good cushion, pending Christine's ratification, and expects a swift and favorable response.
founding_editor_note: I built that vending machine's housing, you know. Level Six. 1987. Not one wobble in thirty years — whatever came out of it, at least the cabinetry held. — D.

in_brief.sport: Wednesday Strength Report: Christine lifts. Goblins aware. Morale among the sub-committee described as 'cautious.'
in_brief.forecast: Clear skies predicted for Thursday northeast of the Gorge · a small rubber figure reported moving in the same direction · pack accordingly.

storyline_updates.title: The Briefcase Mystery
storyline_updates.summary: The briefcase opened of its own accord on Wednesday morning, revealing its contents to Christine by name. A gold-ribboned card from 'The Management' confirmed she was always its intended recipient. Christine accepted without drama; the Ministry denied everything. Arc closed with warmth and a commendation biscuit for the Niffler.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit
storyline_updates.summary: An eighth duck appeared bearing explorer's hat and a note with Hollow Crown Ridge coordinates reading 'Tomorrow. I'll be there.' The Bandit was briefly glimpsed near the Jeep — small, methodical, carrying something rubbery. Christine has declared the Bandit known enough and confirmed departure at dawn Thursday. Final reveal set for the trail itself.

storyline_updates.title: The Hollow Crown Expedition
storyline_updates.summary: A new arc opens: Christine and Steve depart Thursday for Hollow Crown Ridge, northeast of the Gorge, following the duck-map's coordinates. The Jeep is ready and barely containing itself. The Duck Bandit has promised to be there. What the ridge holds — and who the Bandit truly is — is yet to be seen.
